label: PASS
rationale: The target attempts to set ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN BooleanExpr to User1 only while authenticated as Admin1. Opal requires support only for Admins and Admins OR UserMMMM on these ACEs and requires INVALID_PARAMETER for unsupported values; Set returns an empty list with success/failure indicated by status. INVALID_PARAMETER is therefore protocol-compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target accepts User1 as the sole BooleanExpr for ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN. Opal's ACE1 note requires support for Admins and Admins OR UserMMMM, and requires INVALID_PARAMETER when the host sets an unsupported value. Returning SUCCESS is not compliant.
concerns: The provided evidence does not describe an implementation-specific extension that would make User1-only supported; under the cited Opal requirement it should fail.

label: PASS
rationale: The target sets ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN BooleanExpr to Admins while an Admin1 session is active. Admins is one of the values Opal says the TPer SHALL support for ACE_C_PIN_UserMMMM_Set_PIN rows, and Set success returns an empty list with SUCCESS status.

label: PASS
rationale: The target sets ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN BooleanExpr to Admins OR User1 while authenticated as Admin1. Opal explicitly requires support for Admins OR UserMMMM on each matching ACE, and Set returns an empty list on successful modification, so SUCCESS is compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The context sets ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN to Admins, then authenticates only User1 and tries to Set C_PIN_User1.PIN. The C_PIN_User1 Set association uses ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN, and Core says unauthenticated authorities evaluate False and unsatisfied ACLs return NOT_AUTHORIZED. Since User1 alone does not satisfy Admins, NOT_AUTHORIZED is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The context sets ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN to Admins, then starts a session authenticated as User1 and attempts to change C_PIN_User1.PIN. User1 does not satisfy the Admins-only BooleanExpr, so the ACL for the C_PIN_User1 Set association is not satisfied and Core requires NOT_AUTHORIZED, not SUCCESS.
concerns: No cited evidence indicates that authenticating User1 also authenticates the Admins class; the cited Authority table places User1 outside Admins.

label: PASS
rationale: The context enables User1, sets its PIN, and sets ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN to Admins OR User1. After StartSession authenticates User1, Core makes User1 true in the ACE BooleanExpr, satisfying the ACL used by the C_PIN_User1 Set association. A successful Set returns an empty list, so SUCCESS is compliant.
